Laserpitium latifolium is a plant species in the genus Laserpitium, which is a group of flowering plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is a perennial plant with health benefits attributed to its ability to aid digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Laserpitium species include alkaloids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Laserpitium species include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat digestive issues and other health problems.

Laserocarpum pseudomeum's classification reflects its relationship to other plants in the Celastraceae family.
